name: Umayyah ibn Saad
title: Warrior of Tayy
shortDescription: A brave Shia of Kufa who joined the Imam upon hearing of his arrival in Karbala and was martyred on Ashura.
fullStory:
Umayyah ibn Sa'd al-Ta'i was a renowned brave horseman of Kufa and a close friend of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS).

He lived in Kufa, but when he heard that Imam Hussain (AS) was surrounded by Ibn Sa'd's army in Karbala, he could not bear it and secretly fled Kufa at night to reach Karbala.

Umayyah joined the Imam's camp in the days before Ashura. On Ashura, with the start of the enemy's first attack, he stood manfully alongside the Imam's devoted companions.

After a brave fight against the Kufan archers, Umayyah ibn Sa'd fell in blood shoulder-to-shoulder with other loyal companions, and his soul ascended to the highest heaven. His name is mentioned in the Ziyarat al-Nahiya al-Muqaddasa.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى أُمَيَّةَ بْنِ سَعْدٍ الطّائِيِّ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon Umayyah ibn Sa'd al-Ta'i...
